source_claim.locator: Figure 3, Equation 24, and Section 5.2, PDF pages 7-8

capture.command: out=docs/dev_tasks/fbf_exact_coulomb_friction/assets/paper_evidence/fig03_backspin; args=(--scene fbf_paper_backspin --headless --steps 130 --width 1920 --height 720 --headless-sidecar $out/timeline.json); for step in {0..130}; do args+=(--headless-shot-at $step:$out/frames/frame_$(printf '%03d' $step).png); done; build/default/cpp/Release/bin/dart-demos ${args[@]}
capture.note: The sidecar runtime_command records the fully expanded 131-shot invocation. Every image came from one deterministic world run.

orange_centroid_method: For each inspected 1920x720 PNG, scan the rendered-world region x=260..1559 and y=58..564; retain pixels with r>=95, 35<=g<=150, b<=75, and r>1.45*g; report the arithmetic mean x. This is a coarse, reproducible direction check only.

visual_claims.claim: The DART sphere first advances and then reverses direction by the paper's five requested instants.
visual_claims.support: The inspected orange-sphere image centroid moves right from x=964.54 at step 0 to x=1275.37 at step 50, then left to x=706.29 at step 120 and x=597.11 at step 130. This is an image-space direction check, not a replacement for the physical trajectory regression.

visual_claims.claim: The shown trajectory used exact FBF without a boxed-LCP fallback.
visual_claims.support: The timeline sidecar reports 130 exact solves, zero exact failures, zero boxed-LCP fallbacks, and residual below 1e-6 at every post-initial captured state.

visual_claims.claim: The five stills are synchronized to Figure 3's displayed times.
visual_claims.support: Steps 0, 10, 50, 120, and 130 at dt=1/60 map to 0, 0.1667, 0.8333, 2.0, and 2.1667 seconds, labeled as 0, 0.17, 0.83, 2.0, and 2.17 seconds.

image_verification.strict_contrast_note: The optional strict gate rejects these dark renders: sampled luminance spread ranges from 4.7 on the cropped sheet to 27.9 on the full host frames, below the generic 90.0 threshold, with additional bright/mid-tone count failures on some images. The failures are retained rather than hidden; the sphere, ground, timestamps, and exact-solver diagnostics are visually legible in all six inspected images.

composition.command: python3 scripts/image_compose.py side-by-side panels/frame_000.png panels/frame_010.png panels/frame_050.png panels/frame_120.png panels/frame_130.png --out fig03_dart_fbf_5panel.png --labels 't=0.00 s (step 0)' 't=0.17 s (step 10)' 't=0.83 s (step 50)' 't=2.00 s (step 120)' 't=2.17 s (step 130)' --gap 8 --manifest fig03_dart_fbf_5panel.compose.json

not_claimed: No Kamino image, trajectory, or performance parity is claimed.
No MuJoCo image, trajectory, or performance parity is claimed.
No pixel-diff against a paper-author-approved golden is claimed.
No airborne landing phase is claimed; the current DART fixture starts the sphere at z=0.245 m with radius 0.25 m against a plane whose top is z=0.
The DART-only GIF is not a reproduction of the source video's narration, slow-motion edit, or three-solver layout.
remaining_blockers: A faithful runnable Kamino comparison is unavailable locally.
The existing MuJoCo harness has not been rendered through the same five-instant camera/layout contract.
There is no approved paper-source golden/reference policy for a pixel diff.
The semantic 00:02-00:24 source-video comparison needs matched Ours, MuJoCo, and Kamino rows plus slow motion.
